name: Monstera integrifolia
aboutText: Monstera integrifolia is a rare climbing aroid native to the humid wet tropical forests of Colombia and Panama. It is distinguished by its consistently entire, non-fenestrated leaves, which sets it apart from the more common lobed Monstera species. The plant exhibits a refined climbing architecture, using aerial roots to anchor itself into rich, organic substrates while ascending from the shaded understory toward filtered light. It thrives in high-humidity environments where consistent moisture is maintained.

morphology.venation: Pronounced midrib with subtle lateral veins
morphology.growthHabit: Climbing with visible nodes and aerial roots

fieldNotes.title: The Whole Leaf, Sometimes
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Integrifolia means 'entire-leaved' — a reference to the fact that, in some growth stages and populations, the leaves lack the fenestrations one associates with the genus. An interesting botanical commentary on the developmental plasticity of Monstera leaf form: fenestration is not fixed but varies with the growth stage, light environment, and individual genetics. A Colombia and Panama species at accessible middle-tier pricing. Not the most dramatic Monstera in the collection but a useful illustration of how varied leaf expression within a single genus can be.
